data.title: South Korea and Japan Finance Ministers Agree to Hold Next Round of Talks Within a Year
data.description: South Korea's Ministry of Finance announced that finance ministers from South Korea and Japan have agreed to conduct the next round of dialogue within approximately one year. According to Jin10, this decision marks a continuation of efforts to strengthen economic cooperation between the two nations. The agreement was reached during recent discussions aimed at addressing mutual economic interests and challenges. Both countries are looking to enhance bilateral relations and explore opportunities for collaboration in various sectors. The upcoming talks are expected to focus on key economic issues and strategies for fostering regional stability and growth.

data.title: South Korea and Japan Praise Efforts to Improve Market Access for Japanese Institutional Investors
data.description: South Korea's Ministry of Finance has announced that financial leaders from South Korea and Japan have expressed appreciation for South Korea's efforts to enhance market access for Japanese institutional investors. According to Jin10, the discussions between the two nations focused on strengthening economic ties and facilitating smoother investment processes. The collaboration aims to foster a more inclusive financial environment, benefiting both countries' economies. This development marks a significant step in improving bilateral relations and promoting mutual growth in the financial sector.

data.title: Polymarket estimates the probability of BTC exceeding $72,000 today is only 4%, after it briefly surged to $74,000 last night before retreating.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer's monitoring, in Polymarket's prediction event that "Bitcoin price will be above x USD on March 14th," the probability of BTC exceeding $72,000 today is currently only 4%, and the probability of exceeding $74,000 is less than 1%; the probability of exceeding $70,000 is also only 81% (lower than the current market price). Last night, Bitcoin's price surged to nearly $74,000 before quickly falling back and is currently fluctuating around $70,600, with the related prediction market probability clearly cooling down. Odaily Seer: Seeing the changes before pricing.

data.title: Japan's Finance Minister Vows to Address Currency Market Volatility
data.description: Japan's Finance Minister, Goto Katayama, has announced that the government is prepared to respond promptly to fluctuations in the currency market. According to Jin10, Katayama emphasized the importance of monitoring the impact of exchange rate changes on the daily lives of citizens. The statement highlights the government's commitment to maintaining economic stability amid potential market volatility.

data.title: Polymarket's US platform reported a nominal transaction volume of $750 million and over 5 million transactions.
data.description: Polymarket's US platform reported a nominal trading volume of $750 million and over 5 million transactions. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Copper Deficits May Persist for Decades, New Supply Faces Delays
data.description: Copper deficits could continue for the next 25 years, according to NS3.AI. The report highlights that bringing new mine supply online may take approximately 18 years. By 2050, deficits might reach around 80% of current production levels, posing significant challenges for the industry.

data.title: Drone Attack Halts Oil Loading Operations at Fujairah Port
data.description: Oil loading activities at the port of Fujairah, located near the Strait of Hormuz, have been suspended following a drone attack and subsequent fire on Saturday morning, according to sources familiar with the situation. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of the incident on the region's oil operations. The attack has raised concerns about the security of oil transportation in this strategic area, which is a critical passage for global oil shipments. Authorities are currently assessing the situation to determine the extent of the damage and potential disruptions to oil supply routes.

data.title: Iran Claims to Have Downed U.S. MQ-9 Drone Near Abbas Port
data.description: Iran has reportedly claimed responsibility for shooting down a U.S. MQ-9 drone near the port of Abbas, according to Jin10. This development adds to the ongoing tensions between the two nations, with potential implications for regional stability. The incident underscores the fragile geopolitical landscape in the area, as both countries navigate complex diplomatic and military interactions. Further details on the incident and its repercussions are yet to be disclosed.

data.title: Dubai's War Impact Highlights Capital Mobility Lessons from Hong Kong's Covid-Era Expat Exodus
data.description: The ongoing conflict in Dubai underscores the rapid relocation of capital, drawing parallels with Hong Kong's expatriate departure during the Covid era. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how geopolitical tensions can swiftly influence financial movements. The situation in Dubai serves as a reminder of the volatility in global capital flows, similar to the shifts witnessed in Hong Kong when expatriates left amid pandemic-related challenges. This comparison illustrates the fragile nature of economic stability in regions affected by external pressures.

data.title: Iran Reports Damage to Cultural Sites Amid Recent Conflicts
data.description: Iran's Ministry of Cultural Heritage, Tourism, and Handicrafts announced on March 14 that recent conflicts have led to significant structural damage to at least 56 museums, historical sites, and cultural landmarks across several provinces. According to Jin10, the attacks were attributed to the United States and Israel. Tehran reported the highest number of affected sites, with 19 locations damaged, while Kurdistan Province saw 12 important historical sites impacted.

data.title: Bitdeer Reports Zero Bitcoin Holdings Despite Mining Output
data.description: Bitdeer, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company, has released its latest Bitcoin holdings data on the X platform. According to ChainCatcher, as of the week ending March 13, Bitdeer continues to maintain zero Bitcoin holdings. During this period, the company produced 158.8 BTC through mining operations but sold an equivalent amount of 158.8 BTC.

data.title: JSW Steel Secures Coal Mine Development Rights in Mozambique
data.description: JSW Steel, India's largest steel producer by capacity, has obtained the rights to develop a coal mine in Mozambique's Tete province. Bloomberg posted on X that this acquisition grants JSW Steel access to an essential raw material for steel production. The move is expected to bolster the company's resource base and support its manufacturing operations. Mozambique's Tete province is known for its rich coal deposits, making it a strategic location for JSW Steel's expansion efforts. This development aligns with the company's strategy to secure key resources and enhance its production capabilities.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Swiss Franc Strengthens Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: The ongoing conflict in Iran has led to increased investment in haven assets, resulting in the Swiss franc reaching its strongest level against the euro and the dollar in over ten years. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact on local businesses, which are now facing difficult decisions due to the currency's appreciation. The situation underscores the broader economic implications of geopolitical tensions, as firms navigate the challenges posed by a stronger franc.

data.title: Aave Releases Aave V4 Ethereum Mainnet Activation Governance Proposal
data.description: Aave founder Stani.eth announced on the X platform that Aave has released the Aave V4 Ethereum mainnet activation governance proposal. Aave V4 will introduce a modular architecture, in which Liquidity Hubs will hold shared liquidity, while Spokes will define independent lending environments with governance restrictions. This version is also expected to launch the topology, rollout path, implementation and control models, and the initial asset range for risk parameterization. If the community reaches a consensus on the proposal, a Snapshot will be submitted for voting.

data.title: Bitdeer sold 158.8 BTC this week and currently maintains a zero holding.
data.description: Bitdeer, a Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company, released its latest Bitcoin holdings data on the X platform. As of the week ending March 13, it maintained zero Bitcoin holdings. This week, its Bitcoin mining output was 158.8 BTC, but it also sold 158.8 BTC during the same period.

data.title: China Sees Record Domestic Travel During Spring Festival
data.description: Chinese travelers embarked on a record number of domestic trips during the Spring Festival travel season. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the surge in travel activity across the country. The Spring Festival, a significant cultural event, traditionally sees increased movement as people visit family and friends. This year, the number of trips reached unprecedented levels, reflecting a robust demand for domestic travel. The increase in travel is seen as a positive indicator for the country's tourism sector, which has been recovering from previous disruptions. The Spring Festival travel season is a crucial period for the tourism industry, and the record numbers suggest a strong rebound in domestic travel.

data.title: Crypto Startups Secure $192 Million in Weekly Fundraising
data.description: Crypto startups have successfully raised $192 million this week, contributing to a total of $2.75 billion in fundraising for the year. According to NS3.AI, Kast led the funding efforts with an $80 million Series A round aimed at enhancing stablecoin payment solutions.

data.title: UAE Airlines Recovering Capacity Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description: The United Arab Emirates' General Civil Aviation Authority announced on Friday, the 13th, that adjustments to air transport due to the situation in the Middle East are being reversed. According to Ming Pao, UAE airlines have restored their capacity to 44.6% of pre-conflict levels.

data.title: Australia Releases Emergency Fuel Reserves Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
On March 14, the Australian government announced the release of approximately six days' worth of gasoline and five days' worth of diesel from its emergency fuel reserves. According to BlockBeats, this marks the first time the reserves have been tapped since Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

Australian Energy Minister Chris Bowen stated that the decision aims to provide fuel suppliers with greater flexibility in the context of the Middle East conflict. Currently, Australia maintains reserves of about 36 days of gasoline, 29 days of aviation fuel, and 32 days of diesel.

This action is part of a broader initiative by the International Energy Agency (IEA), involving 32 member countries releasing a total of 400 million barrels of oil reserves to stabilize global oil prices and ensure supply. Additionally, the Australian government has temporarily relaxed fuel quality standards, anticipating an extra supply of approximately 100 million liters of gasoline per month over the next two months.

The recent surge in international oil prices, influenced by the situation in the Middle East and disruptions in shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, has led to fuel supply shortages in some parts of Australia.

data.title: Iran is reportedly considering allowing some oil tankers to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, provided that the oil shipments are settled in RMB.
data.description: On March 13 local time, CNN, citing a senior Iranian official, reported that Iran is considering allowing a limited number of oil tankers to pass through the Strait of Hormuz, provided that the oil cargoes carried by these tankers are settled in yuan. The source added that Iran is currently working on a new plan to manage the passage of oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, and the potential move is being considered in this context. CNN noted that current international oil trade is almost entirely settled in US dollars, with the only exception being sanctioned Russian oil, which is traded in rubles or yuan. (Observer Network)
